skill.name: azure-rbac
skill.description: Helps users find the right Azure RBAC role for an identity with least privilege access, then generate CLI commands and Bicep code to assign it. USE FOR: "what role should I assign", "least privilege role", "RBAC role for", "role to read blobs", "role for managed identity", "custom role definition", "assign role to identity". DO NOT USE FOR: creating or configuring managed identities, or general Azure security hardening; those are out of scope for this role-selection skill.
skill.summary: Find minimal Azure RBAC roles and generate assignment guidance.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported four high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but review of SKILL.md found no cryptographic algorithms, hashing, encryption, or executable code. The alerts are false positives against Azure RBAC guidance text and tool names; no prompt injection or data exfiltration intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static alert at line 3 points to YAML description text. No weak cryptographic algorithm or crypto operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Very low confidence in a security risk because the line starts a plain description block. No crypto API, algorithm name, or executable behavior appears there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The first static alert at line 8 points to instructions for Azure documentation and role-assignment helper tools. No weak cryptographic algorithm is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Very low confidence in a security risk because the text describes Azure RBAC lookup and generation steps. It contains no crypto implementation or sensitive data handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The second duplicate static alert at line 8 has the same benign context. It describes generating CLI commands and Bicep snippets for RBAC assignments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Very low confidence in a security risk because this is a duplicate alert on non-executable guidance. No algorithm selection or cryptographic primitive is referenced.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The third duplicate static alert at line 8 is unsupported by semantic review. The line asks the assistant to use Azure tools for least-privilege RBAC guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Very low confidence in a security risk because the line contains workflow guidance only. No prompt injection, obfuscation, exfiltration, or crypto weakness was found.

content.user_title: Choose Azure RBAC Roles with Least Privilege
content.value_statement: Azure role assignment is risky when permissions are broader than needed. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code select minimal RBAC roles and produce assignment guidance.
actual_capabilities: Finds built-in Azure RBAC roles that match requested permissions.
Prioritizes least-privilege role selection for an identity.
Generates Azure CLI command guidance for role assignment.
Suggests custom role definitions when no built-in role fits.
Provides Bicep role-assignment snippets using Azure schema guidance.
Uses Azure best-practice guidance when preparing infrastructure examples.
limitations: Does not create or configure managed identities.
Does not perform broad Azure security hardening reviews.
Depends on available Azure documentation and helper tools for current role details.
Provides guidance and snippets, but does not grant access directly.

use_cases.title: Assign Storage Reader Access
use_cases.description: Identify the minimal role needed for a service identity to read blobs without write permissions.

use_cases.title: Prepare Infrastructure Templates
use_cases.description: Generate Bicep guidance for repeatable RBAC assignments in deployment workflows.

use_cases.title: Review Access Requests
use_cases.description: Compare a requested permission set with built-in Azure roles before approving access.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Basic Role
prompt_templates.prompt: What Azure RBAC role should I assign so this managed identity can read blobs in one storage account?

prompt_templates.title: Compare Least-Privilege Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare built-in Azure RBAC roles for reading Key Vault secrets. Recommend the least-privilege role and explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Assignment Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the minimal RBAC role for a workload identity to write messages to a Service Bus queue. Generate Azure CLI assignment guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Custom Role
prompt_templates.prompt: No built-in role should allow these exact permissions. Draft a custom Azure RBAC role definition and Bicep assignment guidance for review.

output_examples.input: A managed identity needs to read blobs in one storage account.
output_examples.output: The response recommends a narrow storage data reader role, explains why broader roles are unnecessary, and outlines assignment steps.

output_examples.input: A deployment pipeline needs repeatable RBAC setup for a resource group.
output_examples.output: The response describes the selected role, target scope, principal details needed, and a Bicep-based assignment approach.

output_examples.input: No built-in role exactly matches a limited permission list.
output_examples.output: The response suggests a custom role definition for review and explains how to assign it after approval.

best_practices: Provide the identity type, target scope, and exact permissions needed.
Ask for built-in role options before requesting a custom role.
Review generated role assignments with your Azure governance process before deployment.
anti_patterns: Requesting broad owner or contributor access when only data-plane access is needed.
Using the skill for managed identity creation or configuration.
Applying generated assignments without checking the intended Azure scope.

faq.question: Can this skill assign Azure roles for me?
faq.answer: No. It generates guidance, commands, and Bicep snippets that you can review and run in your environment.

faq.question: Does it support custom Azure RBAC roles?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest a custom role definition when no built-in role matches the requested permissions.

faq.question: Can it create managed identities?
faq.answer: No. Creating or configuring managed identities is outside this skill's scope.

faq.question: How does it choose least-privilege access?
faq.answer: It compares the requested permissions with Azure role definitions and selects the narrowest matching role.

faq.question: Can it generate Bicep examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It can provide Bicep role-assignment snippets using Azure schema and best-practice guidance.

faq.question: Is this a full Azure security review tool?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on Azure RBAC role selection and assignment guidance.
